title: Aeo Competitor Arc
html:
The standard reading of rankett's strategy is "Yahir picked white-label because agencies are a leveraged channel." That's true but underspecified. The richer reading: between Sept 22 and Nov 18, 2025, Yahir lived through a 9-week competitive collision where the B2C version of his AEO product showed up on the open market, fully funded, at half the price he was about to charge — and the white-label pivot is the strategic answer to that collision. This page walks the timeline from rankett.md through the screenshot record so the graph can navigate it.

Through-line

In one sentence: Yahir spent September designing a $45/mo direct-to-SMB AEO offer; spent late October watching Peekaboo ship the productized $100–200/mo version of a similar offer; and by January 2026 had repositioned rankett above the fight at $998/seat/month sold only to agencies with 5+ existing clients. The Peekaboo collision is the load-bearing inflection point — without it, Rankett is probably still a B2C dashboard. The screenshots between Oct 23 and Oct 24 — Yahir's Insight AI funnel annotation on top of a ScoreApp template the evening of Oct 23 (third-party / source: ScoreApp funnel-builder; the OCR's first labeled element reads **Use ScoreApp to build funnel**), then the Peekaboo homepage screenshotted at 4:15 AM on Oct 24 — capture the moment Yahir realized someone else had already shipped a productized version of the AEO funnel he was just beginning to scope. Yahir was annotating a generic funnel-builder template, not drawing his own version of Peekaboo.

The competitive landscape (Sept–Dec 2025)

The category Yahir was entering had at least four named players by the time he locked in his positioning:

  • Peekaboo — the direct collision. AI-visibility SaaS, agency tiers ($100/$200/custom), founders john rice (CTO) + filipe lins duarte (CEO). Shipped, has logos (Panasonic, Atlas, Intellibright), 3,000+ users badge, fully-built dashboard with daily prompt tracking and competitor benchmarking. The "trusted by" row was already past-traction by Oct 24.
  • AEOchecker.ai — the lead-discovery tool Yahir was using himself to surface visibility gaps in prospect websites per the Sept 22 offer draft. Free utility / freemium signal-generator. Not really a competitor; the tool that proved the gap exists.
  • ScoreApp — the funnel-builder Yahir designed Insight AI on top of per the Oct 23 funnel sketch: free quiz → score → competitor compare → upsell ladder ($14.99 DIY → $99 DFY → $500-1000 Premium). Not a direct competitor; it's the platform layer Yahir was going to wrap.
  • Semrush — the incumbent. Announced "Semrush One" — unified SEO + AI-search visibility — the same week Yahir was reverse-engineering Peekaboo. The incumbent move is the "this category is real and the big SEO tool is now playing in it" signal. Worse for any sub-scale challenger trying to win on features.
  • An unnamed "For Brands / For Agencies" tool — captured in the same evening competitor scan. Already pitching white-label reports for agencies. The "manage client AI visibility at scale" framing is the proof-by-existence that the agency-channel idea was being floated by others too.

The category Yahir is entering, by Oct 2025, has: a funded direct-competitor (Peekaboo) at the SMB tier, an incumbent (Semrush) bundling AEO into existing SEO suites, at least one nameless agency-channel player already pitching the white-label angle, plus utility tools (AEOchecker) people use to self-diagnose. It is a real market with a real shape, not a greenfield.

The Peekaboo collision moment — Oct 24, 4:15 AM

The exact sequence, by timestamp:

  • Oct 18, 11:25 PM — Yahir + Diddles iMessage thread: explicit "the path seems too obvious" — they decide to go all-in on AEO for local SMBs. Funnel design under debate. Pricing thesis emerges: 2-week free trial → $99/month. Diddles closes; Yahir does backend.
  • Oct 23, 7:33 PM — Insight AI funnel annotation on a ScoreApp template: the screenshot is Yahir's annotation layer on top of a ScoreApp funnel-builder template (top of OCR: **Use ScoreApp to build funnel**) (third-party / source: ScoreApp). The annotated quiz template includes "Your AI Presence score is 8/100" + FOMO ("99% of businesses don't talk in AI"), tiered pricing $14.99 / $99 / $500-1000. The pricing structure and dynamic-result language are ScoreApp's; Yahir's contribution is the AEO/Insight-AI overlay.
  • Oct 23, 7:34 PM — Insight AI dashboard mockup: visual upsell, twin Business + Competitor visibility tests, paywall.
  • Oct 23, 7:40 PM — Competitor landing scan: "For Brands / For Agencies" white-label tool. (Yahir is already scanning the field while drafting.)
  • Oct 24, 4:15 AM — Peekaboo discovery. Full landing page + dashboard screenshot. Slack scoring 75/100, top citation opportunities, ~9,500 monthly visitors, competitor traffic for Zoom/Teams/Google Chat/Discord. 8.5 hours after Yahir was annotating a ScoreApp funnel-builder template for the same product category, Peekaboo's homepage showed someone else had already shipped a productized version of that idea. Note: the Oct 24 4:15 AM timestamp is Austin local time per file metadata (2025-10-24T04:15:22-0500) — this one is genuinely late-night.
  • Oct 28, 10:16–10:17 AM — Peekaboo landing re capture + pricing tear down ($100/$200/custom). Re-screenshotted likely to share with diddles for the strategic conversation.
  • Oct 29, 1:37 PM — Semrush One announcement clipped. The incumbent is moving in.
  • Oct 29, 8:54 PM — Cleaner Peekaboo pricing re shot. Yahir is still studying the tier card.
  • Oct 30, 2:03 AM — Inside the Peekaboo product. Onboarding flow: auto-suggest 10 Discovery / Purchase Intent / Sentiment prompts, language English, location US. Yahir is reverse-engineering the AI-visibility tracking architecture from inside the trial at 2 AM.
  • Oct 31, 10:18 PM — Peekaboo founder bios cropped. Same CTO/CEO split Yahir is mirroring with diddles. The team-shape recognition is explicit.
  • Nov 18, 11:33 PM — Visibility decline trend report: tracked site dropped 63 → 48 (−23.8%), with auto-prescribed action plan. Confirms the monthly-report-as-recurring-value core of what an AEO product actually sells.

The cluster from Oct 23 (own funnel sketch) through Nov 18 (validated value loop in someone else's product) is a 27-day live competitive education. By the time Rankett's GTM bible is written six weeks later, every defensive choice in it (white-label-only, agency-channel-only, $998/seat above Peekaboo's $200, 60 day activation rule to filter tire-kickers) is a direct response to what the Oct 23–Nov 18 surveillance taught.

What differentiates Rankett

Read against the competitive map, rankett's positioning is defined by what Peekaboo and Semrush are not:

  • Channel: Peekaboo sells direct to brands/agencies via self-serve. Semrush sells direct to in-house marketers. Rankett refuses both — agency-only, never end-client, never self-serve. The ghost white label architecture is impossible for either competitor to copy without rebuilding their entire product UX.
  • Price: Peekaboo $100–200/mo per agency. Rankett $998/seat/mo, with the agency reselling at $3K–$6K to the end client. Rankett is positioned 5× above Peekaboo on price and structurally below Peekaboo in customer count — it's a smaller TAM with much higher per-account revenue, which is what an under-resourced solo founder can actually service.
  • Filter: Peekaboo will sell to anyone who clicks. Rankett requires 5+ existing clients to qualify per the GTM bible, and the 60 day activation rule kicks tire-kickers off the platform if they haven't onboarded one paying client in 60 days. The filter is the exclusivity instinct from the China chapter productized.
  • Moat shape: Peekaboo's moat is feature parity + brand. Rankett's moat is pooled data network effect — every new agency benefits from results accumulated across the network, so newer agencies sell with proof from older agencies' data. Whether this is real-in-product or only-in-pitch is an open question per the Rankett page, but the positioning is moat-shaped in a way Peekaboo's isn't.
  • Footprint: Peekaboo has founders + funding + 3,000+ users + logos. Rankett has Yahir + diddles + a leads list and a pre-launch pitch deck. The differentiation has to be structural, not feature-based — Yahir cannot win a build-vs-build race against a funded team. Going up-market to a higher-margin gatekept channel is the only viable answer.

The honest read is: Rankett's whole strategy is the Oct-24 collision processed through the Yahir+Diddles partnership and refracted through the no runway discipline insistence that they can't raise. Peekaboo's existence forced the gatekept-channel choice; the no-raise constraint made the high-ticket-low-volume math the only viable shape; the agency white-label architecture is what those two constraints reduce to.

Open competitive risks

  • Semrush bundling kills the standalone category. If "AEO" gets folded into every SEO suite as a free feature by mid-2026, the agency value-prop ("buy this from us so you don't have to learn AEO") collapses. Rankett's defense has to be operational depth (the 50+ directory management, schema markup, llm.txt implementation) rather than the visibility-tracking metric itself.
  • Peekaboo opens an agency tier. Their existing "Grow" tier ($200, 5 brands, 9,000 data points, white-label feature row) is already 80% of an agency offering. If they layer a true white-label / multi-tenant version on top, Rankett's gatekept-channel moat shrinks fast.
  • The unnamed "For Brands / For Agencies" tool from Oct 23 is unidentified in the corpus. Worth disambiguating — could be Peekaboo competitor, could be one of Vazoola-class tools surfacing in 2026.
  • 60 day activation rule hasn't been validated at scale. A filter that turns away most prospects is fine when you have inbound leverage; risky when you're cold-outbounding to fill the funnel.
  • The pooled-data network effect is asserted but not proven per rankett. If competitors notice Rankett's central asset is data-not-product, they can replicate the data layer faster than Rankett can replicate their feature surface.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir or Diddles ever directly engage with Peekaboo as a customer (trial signup, sales call) beyond the surveillance screenshots? The 2 AM onboarding flow screenshot suggests yes — confirm.
  • Is the Sept 22 aeo agency $45/mo offer fully dead, or did it ship to any pilot clients before the pivot? The corpus doesn't show evidence of revenue at that tier.
  • The unnamed "For Brands / For Agencies" white label tool needs identification — possibly the most direct competitor Rankett actually has.
  • Did the Nov 18 visibility-decline trend report belong to a Peekaboo-tracked site, an Insight AI prototype, or a Rankett internal test? Worth distinguishing to know what Yahir's actually tracking by Nov.
  • Was the team-shape recognition (CTO + CEO split mirroring Yahir + Diddles) flagged consciously, or only in retrospect? The founder bio screenshot crop suggests conscious — Yahir was studying the model.

Source summaries

  • 2025 09 22 screenshot 2025 09 22 at 11 30 13 am 2984c5ab — first AEO offer draft, $45/mo + AEOchecker
  • 2025 10 18 screenshot 2025 10 18 at 11 25 17 pm 049c2451 — Yahir + Diddles partnership chat, AEO go-all-in moment
  • 2025 10 23 screenshot 2025 10 23 at 7 33 39 pm 5fd5798f — Insight AI funnel sketch (ScoreApp-built)
  • 2025 10 23 screenshot 2025 10 23 at 7 34 12 pm 6ff06ea0 — Insight AI dashboard mockup
  • 2025 10 23 screenshot 2025 10 23 at 7 40 46 pm 92e6c470 — competitor landing scan, "For Brands / For Agencies"
  • 2025 10 24 screenshot 2025 10 24 at 4 15 22 am f3a7573c — Peekaboo discovery moment, 4:15 AM
  • 2025 10 28 screenshot 2025 10 28 at 10 16 40 am b0dc5ac7 — Peekaboo landing re-capture
  • 2025 10 28 screenshot 2025 10 28 at 10 17 08 am e3eb47b4 — Peekaboo pricing + founder bios
  • 2025 10 29 screenshot 2025 10 29 at 1 37 30 pm 53f7ca64 — Semrush One launch (incumbent move)
  • 2025 10 29 screenshot 2025 10 29 at 8 54 01 pm fa39f428 — cleaner Peekaboo pricing re-shot
  • 2025 10 30 screenshot 2025 10 30 at 2 03 36 am a0ae9d76 — inside Peekaboo onboarding (2 AM reverse engineering)
  • 2025 10 31 screenshot 2025 10 31 at 10 18 33 pm abec172c — Peekaboo founder bios cropped (team-shape recognition)
  • 2025 11 18 screenshot 2025 11 18 at 11 33 13 pm ac095aed — visibility-decline trend report (validated value loop)

Connections

  • rankett — the strategic answer to this collision
  • peekaboo — the direct competitor; the load-bearing entity in this arc
  • insight ai — the project that got displaced by the discovery
  • aeo agency — the pre-pivot positioning ($45/mo direct SMB)
  • ai search optimization — the category itself
  • ai visibility engine — the architectural label for the product class
  • ghost white label — Rankett's structural answer
  • 60 day activation rule — Rankett's filter, defining feature
  • pooled data network effect — Rankett's claimed moat
  • whitelabel strategy — the broader thesis Peekaboo's existence forced
  • diddles — partner; co-witnessed and co-strategized the pivot
  • john rice · filipe lins duarte — Peekaboo founders, Yahir+Diddles template
  • yahir thesis ai search 2022 2026 — the four-year thesis arc this is the Oct-2025 chapter of
  • brotein as rankett prototype — sister synthesis on the predecessor product arc
  • from fitness to software the brotein arc — sister synthesis on the larger Brotein-to-Rankett pivot
  • no runway discipline — the financial constraint that made the up-market positioning the only viable answer
  • exclusivity as chinese trait — the cultural instinct underwriting the gatekept-channel choice
  • the austin thesis — the broader founder context this collision happens inside
